chunks.text: FORMAT Online DATE 26 June 2026 START TIME 2:00 pm END TIME 6:00 pm Access Provided Assets Drive The Objective You are tasked with building a premium, high-converting, responsive landing page for an advanced AI-driven data automation platform. This round is a direct test of your architectural integrity, engineering speed, motion choreography, and SEO hygiene under a strict countdown. Round Details & Constraints Phase 1 Official Problem Statement: Next-Gen AI Platform Speed Run Format: Online→ Allowed Frameworks: React, Next.js, Vue, Nuxt, SolidJS, Angular, or Vanilla JS.→ Styling Tools: Utility CSS (Tailwind CSS, Bootstrap) or custom CSS.→ Submission Requirement:→ Public GitHub repository→ Provided Assets Layout Brief Build a highly cohesive, premium SaaS landing page for an AI automation platform. The exact layout �ow, copy, and visual storytelling are up to your creative discretion. However, the interface must naturally weave together a compelling Hero area, a technical Feature showcase, a structured Pricing tier matrix, and social proof—all while maintaining strict semantic HTML and responsive breakpoint �uidities.

weave together a compelling Hero area, a technical Feature showcase, a structured Pricing tier matrix, and social proof—all while maintaining strict semantic HTML and responsive breakpoint �uidities.weave together a compelling Hero area, a technical Feature showcase, a structured Pricing tier matrix, and social proof—all while maintaining strict semantic HTML and responsive breakpoint �uidities.together a compelling Hero area, a technical Feature showcase, a structured Pricing tier matrix, and social proof—all while maintaining strict semantic HTML and responsive breakpoint �uidities. Core Feature Requirements Feature 1: Matrix-Driven Pricing & Performance-Isolated Currency Switcher Feature 2: Bento-to-Accordion Wrapper with State Persistence Live deployment link→ Demo Video (drive link not exceeding 100MB)→ SVGs: It contains some basic UI elements which should be used while building. You cannot use any external resources. → Font list: Strict typography parameters specifying two primary font families to con�gure via CSS/Head layers. → Color palette: Pre-de�ned hex codes outlining the exact aesthetic constraints.→ demo.mp4: A compressed screen recording detailing the structural layout motion expected for this build. → The Blueprint: A pricing tier component that toggles between Monthly and Annual billing cycles across three currencies: INR (₹), USD ($), and EUR (€).

layout motion expected for this build. → The Blueprint: A pricing tier component that toggles between Monthly and Annual billing cycles across three currencies: INR (₹), USD ($), and EUR (€).layout motion expected for this build. → The Blueprint: A pricing tier component that toggles between Monthly and Annual billing cycles across three currencies: INR (₹), USD ($), and EUR (€).layout motion expected for this build. → The Blueprint: A pricing tier component that toggles between Monthly and Annual billing cycles across three currencies: INR (₹), USD ($), and EUR (€). → The Data Logic: You must compute the �nal values dynamically using a multi-dimensional con�guration object/matrix that factors in a base tier rate, a �at 20% annual discount multiplier, and regional tari� variables. → The Blueprint: Present the core features via a modern Bento-Grid layout on desktop. On mobile viewports, this component must refactor into a �uid, touch-optimized Accordion list. → You Can You Cannot The Context Lock Constraint: If a user is actively hovering over or interacting with a speci�c bento-node on desktop and abruptly resizes the browser window past the mobile breakpoint, your application must programmatically transfer that exact active index context over to the mobile Accordion state, ensuring the corresponding panel is open smoothly upon layout transition.

your application must programmatically transfer that exact active index context over to the mobile Accordion state, ensuring the corresponding panel is open smoothly upon layout transition.your application must programmatically transfer that exact active index context over to the mobile Accordion state, ensuring the corresponding panel is open smoothly upon layout transition.your application must programmatically transfer that exact active index context over to the mobile Accordion state, ensuring the corresponding panel is open smoothly upon layout transition. → Use 3D Frameworks: Libraries like three.js are fully permitted to enhance visual components.→ Deploy Anywhere: Any cloud deployment platform can be used (Vercel, Netlify, GitHub Pages, etc.) as long as a working live link is provided. → Replicate Motion Progressively: Implement the transitions, layout animations, and micro- interactions demonstrated in reference_showcase.mp4 as closely as your architecture permits to score maximum points. → Incorporate Utility CSS: Tailwind CSS, Bootstrap, or pure custom CSS variables are completely welcome. → Do Not Use External UI or Animation Component Libraries: You are strictly barred from installing or using pre-built components or runtime animation engines (e.g., Shadcn, Radix, HeadlessUI, Framer Motion) for Feature 2. All structures and transitions must be written from scratch. → Do Not Trigger Global Re-renders: Changing the currency dropdown or billing toggle must not re-render the parent component or surrounding layout blocks.

transitions must be written from scratch. → Do Not Trigger Global Re-renders: Changing the currency dropdown or billing toggle must not re-render the parent component or surrounding layout blocks.transitions must be written from scratch. → Do Not Trigger Global Re-renders: Changing the currency dropdown or billing toggle must not re-render the parent component or surrounding layout blocks.transitions must be written from scratch. → Do Not Trigger Global Re-renders: Changing the currency dropdown or billing toggle must not re-render the parent component or surrounding layout blocks. You cannot allow global state re�ows; updates must be strictly isolated to the localized DOM text nodes containing the price strings. → Do Not Use Runtime CSS-in-JS Animation Engines: All motion sequences must utilize hardware-accelerated, native CSS Transitions/Animations or the optimized native Web Animations API (WAAPI). → Do Not Exceed Performance Caps: The initial loading sequence and entry animations must not block semantic HTML indexing, delay Time to Interactive (TTI), or exceed a total orchestration timeline of 500ms. → Entrance & Motion Constraints Phase 1 Scoring Matrix (100 Points Total) 1. Logic, Architecture & State Isolation (40 Points) 2. SEO Optimization & Semantic HTML Structure (30 Points) 3.

UI/UX Usability & Motion Matching (30 Points) Micro-interactions (Hovers/Toggles): 150ms – 200ms using an ease-out curve.→ Structural Layout Re�ows: 300ms – 400ms using an ease-in-out curve.→ Feature 1 Completion (15 pts): Dynamic multi-currency pricing calculation using a multi- dimensional matrix without hardcoded UI values. → The Re-render & State Isolation Guardrail (15 pts): Strict evaluation via Chrome DevTools. Full points are awarded only if changing the billing cycle or currency isolates state updates to the targeted text nodes. Points will be docked instantly if global components re�ow. → Feature 2 Completion & Zero-Dependency Rule (10 pts): Responsive Bento-to-Accordion view transition with automatic index context tracking on window resize. The presence of banned external libraries results in an automatic 0/10 for this section.

The presence of banned external libraries results in an automatic 0/10 for this section. → Semantic DOM Layout (15 pts): Clean markup utilizing appropriate semantic tags (<main>, <header>, <section>, etc.) over deep, non-semantic <div> nesting. → SEO Hygiene & Metadata (10 pts): Correct use of standard meta headers, Open Graph (OG) tags, accessible image attributes, and crawlable text nodes. → Loading Sequence Performance (5 pts): Complete execution of the initial loader and entry orchestration within the strict 500ms threshold without delaying Time to Interactive (TTI). → Asset Compliance & Design Polish (15 pts): Meaningful, complete integration of all asset categories from asset_package.zip (SVG Pack, Fontlist, Color Palette). Missing or unused assets will incur heavy point deductions. → Breakpoint Fluidity (10 pts): Clean, �awless layout adaptation across mobile, tablet, and desktop viewports without horizontal clipping or overlapping typography.

will incur heavy point deductions. → Breakpoint Fluidity (10 pts): Clean, �awless layout adaptation across mobile, tablet, and desktop viewports without horizontal clipping or overlapping typography.will incur heavy point deductions. → Breakpoint Fluidity (10 pts): Clean, �awless layout adaptation across mobile, tablet, and desktop viewports without horizontal clipping or overlapping typography.incur heavy point deductions. → Breakpoint Fluidity (10 pts): Clean, �awless layout adaptation across mobile, tablet, and desktop viewports without horizontal clipping or overlapping typography. → Motion Accuracy (5 pts): Polish of hover states, easing con�gurations, and structural replication accuracy from the provided demo.mp4. → Evaluation Warning: Codebases that trigger excessive layout thrashing, component mount- �ashing, or unnecessary global re-renders under Chrome DevTools performance tracking will be heavily penalized. Structure your state carefully. Disquali�cation Criteria Your submission will be immediately disquali�ed and excluded from evaluation if it meets any of the following conditions: Invalid or Broken Repository Link: Providing a broken, non-existent, or private GitHub repository link that prevents the evaluation team from auditing your source code. ! Deployment Failures (404 / 500 Errors): Submitting a live hosted link that resolves to a 404 Not Found, 500 Internal Server Error, or fails to build/load entirely during the evaluation window.

! Deployment Failures (404 / 500 Errors): Submitting a live hosted link that resolves to a 404 Not Found, 500 Internal Server Error, or fails to build/load entirely during the evaluation window.! Deployment Failures (404 / 500 Errors): Submitting a live hosted link that resolves to a 404 Not Found, 500 Internal Server Error, or fails to build/load entirely during the evaluation window.! Deployment Failures (404 / 500 Errors): Submitting a live hosted link that resolves to a 404 Not Found, 500 Internal Server Error, or fails to build/load entirely during the evaluation window. ! Plagiarism & Code Duplication: Submitting codebases that match another contestant's repository, utilizing identical forks, or lifting complete, unmodi�ed boilerplates. ! Empty or Mock Repositories: Submitting a repository that only contains placeholder con�guration templates without actual feature source code. ! Banned Component Libraries: The presence of pre-built UI or animation libraries (e.g., Radix, Shadcn, Tailwind UI, Framer Motion) in your dependency con�guration for the core components. ! Hardcoded Feature Metrics: Hardcoding the structural values for the currency and billing engine instead of mapping a dynamic multi-dimensional con�guration matrix. !

FounderLabs - Google AI Automation Setup Google AI Automation Prerequisites Simple setup guide for building a Google-connected AI agent Goal: By the end, you will have a Google account, Gemini API key, Google Cloud project, OAuth Client ID/Secret, one Google Sheet, and the Sheet ID copied safely. Final Checklist [ ] Google account is ready and logged in. [ ] Gemini API key is generated from Google AI Studio. [ ] Google Cloud project is created and project ID is copied. [ ] Required Google APIs are enabled in the project, especially Google Sheets API. [ ] OAuth consent screen is configured for testing. [ ] OAuth Client ID and Client Secret are created and copied. [ ] One Google Sheet is created manually. [ ] Sheet ID is copied from the Google Sheet URL. 1. Create or Use a Google Account Use the same Google account for Google AI Studio, Google Cloud Console, and Google Sheets. This avoids permission confusion later. 1. Open: https://accounts.google.com/ 2. Click: Create account, or sign in if you already have one. 3. Choose: Personal use or Business based on your requirement. 4. Complete: Name, birthday, password, phone/recovery email if asked. 5.

2. Click: Create account, or sign in if you already have one. 3. Choose: Personal use or Business based on your requirement. 4. Complete: Name, birthday, password, phone/recovery email if asked. 5.2. Click: Create account, or sign in if you already have one. 3. Choose: Personal use or Business based on your requirement. 4. Complete: Name, birthday, password, phone/recovery email if asked. 5.2. Click: Create account, or sign in if you already have one. 3. Choose: Personal use or Business based on your requirement. 4. Complete: Name, birthday, password, phone/recovery email if asked. 5. Save: Write down only the email address. Do not share the password. 2. Get Gemini API Key from Google AI Studio This key is used by your app/agent to call Gemini models. Treat it like a password. 6. Open Google AI Studio API Keys page: https://aistudio.google.com/apikey 7. Sign in: Use the Google account from Step 1. 8. Click: Create API key. 9. Select project: Choose the Google Cloud project you created, or create/select one when prompted. 10. Copy: Copy the API key immediately and store it safely. 11. Optional but recommended: Restrict the key to Gemini API only, if the option appears. 3. Create a Google Cloud Project A Google Cloud project is the container where APIs, OAuth credentials, and usage are managed. 12. Open Google Cloud Console: https://console.cloud.google.com/cloud-resource-manager 13. Click: Create Project. 14. Project name: Use a simple name like ai-google-agent or founderlabs-automation. FounderLabs - Google AI Automation Setup 15.

13. Click: Create Project. 14. Project name: Use a simple name like ai-google-agent or founderlabs-automation. FounderLabs - Google AI Automation Setup 15.13. Click: Create Project. 14. Project name: Use a simple name like ai-google-agent or founderlabs-automation. FounderLabs - Google AI Automation Setup 15.13. Click: Create Project. 14. Project name: Use a simple name like ai-google-agent or founderlabs-automation. FounderLabs - Google AI Automation Setup 15. Location/Billing: For personal/testing use, keep defaults where possible. Billing may be requested for some APIs or usage. 16. Click: Create. 17. Copy: Project ID, not only project name. Example: founderlabs-agent-123456. 4. Enable Required Google APIs Before OAuth works with Google services, enable the APIs your app will use. 18. Open API Library: https://console.cloud.google.com/apis/library 19. Select project: Make sure your new Google Cloud project is selected at the top. 20. Search and enable: Google Sheets API. 21. Optional based on your app: Enable Google Drive API if the app needs to create/list files, Gmail API for Gmail access, Calendar API for calendar access. 5. Configure OAuth Consent Screen OAuth consent is the screen Google shows when your app asks permission to access a user account. 22. Open Google Cloud Console: Go to APIs & Services / Google Auth Platform / OAuth consent screen. 23. Choose user type: For a normal Gmail account or public users, choose External.

a user account. 22. Open Google Cloud Console: Go to APIs & Services / Google Auth Platform / OAuth consent screen. 23. Choose user type: For a normal Gmail account or public users, choose External.a user account. 22. Open Google Cloud Console: Go to APIs & Services / Google Auth Platform / OAuth consent screen. 23. Choose user type: For a normal Gmail account or public users, choose External.a user account. 22. Open Google Cloud Console: Go to APIs & Services / Google Auth Platform / OAuth consent screen. 23. Choose user type: For a normal Gmail account or public users, choose External. For only your Workspace company, choose Internal if available. 24. Fill basic details: App name, user support email, developer contact email. 25. Scopes: Add only what you need. For Sheets automation, use Google Sheets scopes. 26. Test users: Add your own Google email while the app is in testing mode. 27. Save: Publish only when you are ready for real users and verification requirements. 6. Create OAuth Client ID and Client Secret These credentials let users securely connect their Google account to your app. 28. Open Credentials page: https://console.cloud.google.com/apis/credentials 29. Click: Create Credentials -> OAuth client ID. 30. Application type: Choose Web application for a backend/web app. Choose Desktop app only for local desktop/CLI testing. 31. Name: Use something clear like FounderLabs Google Agent Local. 32. Authorized redirect URI: For local testing, your developer may use something like http://localhost:3000/oauth2callback or http://localhost:8080/oauth2callback.

FounderLabs Google Agent Local. 32. Authorized redirect URI: For local testing, your developer may use something like http://localhost:3000/oauth2callback or http://localhost:8080/oauth2callback.FounderLabs Google Agent Local. 32. Authorized redirect URI: For local testing, your developer may use something like http://localhost:3000/oauth2callback or http://localhost:8080/oauth2callback.FounderLabs Google Agent Local. 32. Authorized redirect URI: For local testing, your developer may use something like http://localhost:3000/oauth2callback or http://localhost:8080/oauth2callback. Use the exact URL your app expects. 33. Click: Create. 34. Copy: Client ID and Client Secret. Store them safely. Important: The redirect URI must match exactly in both Google Cloud and your app. Even one extra slash can cause a redirect_uri_mismatch error. 7. Create One Google Sheet Manually 35. Open Google Sheets: https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/create 36. Create: Blank spreadsheet. 37. Rename: Use a simple name like AI Automation Data. 38. Add headers: Example: Date, User Query, AI Response, Status. FounderLabs - Google AI Automation Setup 39. Share access: Keep it private for OAuth user access. If using a service account, share the Sheet with the service account email. 8. Copy the Sheet ID from the URL Open your Google Sheet and look at the browser URL. The Sheet ID is the long text between /d/ and /edit.

account, share the Sheet with the service account email. 8. Copy the Sheet ID from the URL Open your Google Sheet and look at the browser URL. The Sheet ID is the long text between /d/ and /edit.account, share the Sheet with the service account email. 8. Copy the Sheet ID from the URL Open your Google Sheet and look at the browser URL. The Sheet ID is the long text between /d/ and /edit.account, share the Sheet with the service account email. 8. Copy the Sheet ID from the URL Open your Google Sheet and look at the browser URL. The Sheet ID is the long text between /d/ and /edit. Example URL: https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/1AbCDeFGhijkLMNOPqrSTuvwxyz1234567890/ edit#gid=0 Sheet ID to copy: 1AbCDeFGhijkLMNOPqrSTuvwxyz1234567890 Credential Collection Sheet Use this table to collect only the needed values. Keep this page private. Item Where to get it Value to paste into app/.env Google Account Email Google account login GEMINI_API_KEY Google AI Studio API Keys page GOOGLE_CLOUD_PROJECT_ID Google Cloud project dashboard/resource manager GOOGLE_OAUTH_CLIENT_ID Google Cloud Console -> Credentials GOOGLE_OAUTH_CLIENT_SECRE T Google Cloud Console -> Credentials GOOGLE_SHEET_ID Google Sheet URL between /d/ and /edit SHEET_NAME Bottom tab in Google Sheet, usually Sheet1 Security Notes  Never post API keys, Client Secrets, or passwords in WhatsApp groups, screenshots, GitHub, or public docs.  Use a .env file locally and add .env to .gitignore before pushing code.  If a key is leaked, delete/revoke it and create a new one.  Use the minimum OAuth scopes required.

GitHub, or public docs.  Use a .env file locally and add .env to .gitignore before pushing code.  If a key is leaked, delete/revoke it and create a new one.  Use the minimum OAuth scopes required.GitHub, or public docs.  Use a .env file locally and add .env to .gitignore before pushing code.  If a key is leaked, delete/revoke it and create a new one.  Use the minimum OAuth scopes required.or public docs.  Use a .env file locally and add .env to .gitignore before pushing code.  If a key is leaked, delete/revoke it and create a new one.  Use the minimum OAuth scopes required. Do not ask for Gmail/Drive access unless the app truly needs it.  For workshops, ask participants to keep credentials on their own machine and not share them with others.

What to Give to AntiGravity / Developer Share these values securely, preferably in a .env file or private secret input: GEMINI_API_KEY=your_gemini_api_key_here GOOGLE_CLIENT_ID=your_oauth_client_id_here GOOGLE_CLIENT_SECRET=your_oauth_client_secret_here GOOGLE_REDIRECT_URI=http://localhost:3000/oauth2callback GOOGLE_SHEET_ID=your_sheet_id_here GOOGLE_SHEET_NAME=Sheet1 FounderLabs - Google AI Automation Setup Official References  Create a Google Account: https://support.google.com/accounts/answer/27441  Gemini API keys - Google AI for Developers: https://ai.google.dev/gemini-api/docs/api-key  Create Google Cloud projects: https://docs.cloud.google.com/resource-manager/docs/creating-managing- projects  OAuth 2.0 for web server applications: https://developers.google.com/identity/protocols/oauth2/web-server  Google Sheets API spreadsheetId reference: https://developers.google.com/workspace/sheets/api/reference/rest/v4/spreadsheets
